programmes.show_description: Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
(1966) This film's sci-fi, not auteur. Pint-sized medics become voyageurs. Add oceanological; to haematological. Not to mention a hidd'n saboteur. #WorldPoetryDay
(1957) Scott's getting smaller; a nuclear thing. The cat getting bolder, scaring him. Lives in a dollhouse. Longs for a friend. Questions his sanity. Will his shrinking end? #WorldPoetryDay
Tom Hanks and director Paul Greengrass talk about making the tense, real-life thriller Captain Phillips.
(2001) Screwball farce with a well-known cast. There's $2m at stake to be first across the States, while John Cleese bets on all the chases. It's all a bit like Wacky Races. #WorldPoetryDay
(2020) Drama set in Italy. A widower and a divorcee. Father's Robert, Jack's his son. Both estranged since Jack was young. Bonding on a renovation: Tuscan reconciliation. #WorldPoetryDay
(2022) A whodunnit worthy of Agatha Christie: a real murder in the theatre in '53. Can Rockwell and Ronan solve the fatal mishap, while keeping the secrets of The Mousetrap? #WorldPoetryDay
Tom Hanks and director Paul Greengrass talk about making the tense, real-life thriller Captain Phillips.
(2013) Tom Hanks gazes anxiously, from the vessel's bow. Barkhad Abdi's pirates board; he's the captain now. #WorldPoetryDay
(2021) Part horror and part mystery. Couples trapped beside the sea. Ageing exponentially. It's complex but you'll get the gist. And it's Shyamalan, so there's a twist. #WorldPoetryDay
...Filmmaker's Apocalypse: (1991) Documentary charting the problems that plagued the Apocalypse Now shoot, threatening this cinematic landmark, Coppola's career and even one actor's life.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
(1981) Fantasy comedy. David Rappaport stars as the leader of rebel dwarves who kidnap a boy, Craig Warnock, and take him on adventures across time. With Sean Connery and John Cleese.
(1985) Sci-fi adventure starring Dennis Quaid and Louis Gossett Jr. A human and his alien enemy find themselves stranded alone on a hostile planet, where they must work together to survive.

programmes.show_name: The Bridge on the River Kwai
programmes.show_description: (1957) David Lean's Oscar-winning fact-based Second World War classic stars Alec Guinness as the driven commander of a group of POWs building a rail bridge for the Japanese.
(2001) Thriller with Owen Wilson. A US Navy flight officer on a NATO reconnaissance mission has to fend for himself when he's shot down by Serb forces in the former Yugoslavia. Violence.
Alexander Skarsgard, Anya Taylor-Joy and director Robert Eggers talk about making their Viking adventure.
(2021) Action thriller with Bob Odenkirk. He's just a dad who goes to work and takes out the trash. But really he's an ex-assassin about to unleash his true self... and take out the trash.
(1985) Thriller starring Arnold Schwarzenegger. To free his kidnapped daughter, an ex US commando must agree to assassinate a South American president. But he's not as pliant as he seems...
